mired.ponder.analog_computator.header: Computing analog values with Analog Computators
mired.ponder.analog_computator.text_1: Analog Computators perform simple arithmetic operations based on analog input signals
mired.ponder.analog_computator.text_2: By default the output signal is the result of adding both the back-input and side-input
mired.ponder.analog_computator.text_3: Both side inputs act as one input, which ever side has the highest value is "the" side-input
mired.ponder.analog_computator.text_4: The output updates instantly whenever any of the inputs change
mired.ponder.analog_computator.text_5: The computation mode can be changed with the panel
mired.ponder.analog_gate.text_1: Analog Gates only allow analog signals of certain strengths to be passed along
mired.ponder.analog_gate.text_10: But increasing the value now, turns the output off again, since it is greater, when in less than or equal mode
mired.ponder.analog_gate.text_2: By default, Analog Gates are in "Greater than or Equal" mode, this can be changed by right-clicking the block base
mired.ponder.analog_gate.text_3: Using the value panel, the selected signal strength can be selected
mired.ponder.analog_gate.text_5: When the input signal strength is less than the configured value, the output will be 0
mired.ponder.analog_gate.text_7: Once the value is reached, the output signal strength will equal the input
mired.ponder.analog_gate.text_8: Now, right-clicking the base cycles it's mode. Since the input is equal to the selected value, nothing changes
mired.ponder.analog_sr_latch.header: Storing analog signals with Analog SR Latches
mired.ponder.analog_sr_latch.text_1: Analog SR Latches are redstone controllable Analog Levers
mired.ponder.analog_sr_latch.text_2: Analog signals from the back input are stored
mired.ponder.analog_sr_latch.text_3: The output value only changes when a signal from one of the side inputs is given
mired.ponder.analog_sr_latch.text_4: By default, Analog SR Latches are in "On Rising Edge" update mode, where they will only trigger one time -- instantly -- when the side input turned on.
mired.ponder.analog_sr_latch.text_5: So if the back input value changes after the initial powering, the value no longer updates
mired.ponder.analog_sr_latch.text_6: This can be changed by right-clicking the Analog SR Latch with a Wrench, switching it to "Continuous" update mode
mired.ponder.analog_sr_latch.text_7: Now when the side input remains turned on, change of the back input, is reflected in the output
mired.ponder.analog_sr_latch.text_8: Unlike "On Rising Edge" mode "Continuous" mode has a 2 tick (1 redstone tick) delay, which can be useful for circuits chaining or back-feeding Analog SR Latches
mired.ponder.analog_sr_latch.text_9: While not being continuously updated, the state of Analog SR Latches can be modified by right-clicking to increase the value and sneak-right-clicking to decrease the value
